Continued Hearing On Plaintiff'S Motion To Designate Previously Produced Documents As Confidential
Off Calendar Dec-15-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to designate previously produced documents as confidential is granted. No opposition has been filed. Comm. Bruce E. Chan.

MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Dec-05-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lindsey E. Blenkhorn for defendant and moving party: McDermott Will & Emery, LLP.
Charles N. Bendes for plaintiff and opposing party: Mansour Salame
COURT REPORTER: Michele Trahan CSR #4017

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion to compel production of documents is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to the documents identified as #8 - 14, and 24 in plaintiff's privilege log. The Court does not find an express waiver of the attorney client privilege; however, the Court will find an implied waiver as the communications relate to the claims in controversy. The motion is denied as to #1, 2, 5, and 6 since the communications occurred after the filing of the amended return. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Pltfs Notice Of Motion Seeking An Order Designating Certain Documents As Confidential
Off Calendar Nov-30-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to designate previously produced documents as confidential is: continued to December 15, 2005 at 10:30 a.m. pursuant to stipulation of parties. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-15-2005 Set For Trial On May-22-06 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On May-05-06 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 503

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-22-2005 Continued To Nov-28-05 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Produce Documents In Response To Request For Production Of Documents At Deposition And For Monetary Sanctions; Compendium Of Federal And Unpublished California Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Sep-22-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Fogelman and Lindsey El Blenkhorn for defendant and moving party: McDermott Will & Emery, LLP
Rod Develbiss for plaintiff and opposing party: Mansour Salame

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Defendant's motion to compel production of documents is granted in part. The motion is granted as to requests 8, 9, 11, 50 - 52, 58, 63. The motion is denied as to requests 45, 60, and 65 as the requests are overbroad and not limited to the other Derivium related transactions. The Court makes no rulings as to the propriety of the assertions of privilege by plaintiff in the log submitted after the filing of the instant motion. The Court finds that plaintiff unduly delayed production of responsive documents and preparation of a privilege log thereby requiring the filing of the instant motion. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1500 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.

MODIFICATION: THAT #45 AND 60 WITHDRAWN.
COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2005 Set For Order To Show Cause On Sep-26-05 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212
